content.problemStatement:
Chef went to Australia and saw the destruction caused by bushfires, which made him sad, so he decided to help the animals by feeding them fruits. First, he went to purchase fruits from Pintu.

Pintu sells MMM different types of fruits (numbered 111 through MMM). He sells them in NNN baskets (numbered 111 through NNN), where for each valid iii, the iii-th basket costs PiP_iPi​ and it contains fruits of type FiF_iFi​. Chef does not have too much money, so he cannot afford to buy everything; instead, he wants to choose one of the MMM available types and purchase all the baskets containing fruits of that type.

Help Chef choose the type of fruit to buy such that he buys at least one basket and the total cost of the baskets he buys is the smallest possible.
constraints: 1≤Fi≤M1 \le F_i \le M1≤Fi​≤M for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers F1,F2,…,FNF_1, F_2, \ldots, F_NF1​,F2​,…,FN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum price Chef must pay.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

The sum of all baskets with fruits of type 111 is 777.
The sum of all baskets with fruits of type 222 is 555.
The sum of all baskets with fruits of type 333 is 101010.
The sum of all baskets with fruits of type 444 is 000 because there are no such baskets.

Chef can only choose fruits of type 111, 222 or 333. Therefore, the minimum price he has to pay is 555.
